articles.title: Show HN: AgentKeeper – cognitive persistence layer for AI agents
articles.description:
Hi HN,

I built AgentKeeper to solve a fundamental problem with AI agents: memory persistence.

Today, agents lose memory when:

• switching providers • restarting • crashing

AgentKeeper introduces a cognitive persistence layer that stores facts independently of any LLM provider and reconstructs context dynamically.

It works across:

• OpenAI • Anthropic • Gemini • Ollama

Memory survives provider switches and restarts.

GitHub: https://github.com/Thinklanceai/agentkeeper

I'm curious if others have faced the same problem and how you're handling memory persistence today.

articles.title: Show HN: Sift – 10-12 HN discussions daily, ranked by practitioner depth
articles.description:
sift scores HN threads by the quality of their discussion, not vote count. It looks for depth (comments at 3+ levels), practitioner markers (experience phrases, code blocks, specific tool names), author diversity, and reference density. Threads that are mostly flames or pile-ons get filtered out.

Updated 4x/day. Shows 6 above the fold, rest behind a tap. Yesterday's picks are gone — no archive, no backlog.

The entire thing is a static page. Astro build, self-hosted fonts, no JS bundles, no tracking. Runs on Cloudflare Pages free tier.

Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47217197

Points: 1
